executiveSummary.text: Cencora (formerly AmerisourceBergen) offers defensive pharmaceutical distribution exposure with a high-quality specialty pharmacy growth engine, but the absence of reported P/E data and thin public float limit investment precision.

businessModel.text: Cencora is the second-largest US pharmaceutical distributor (behind McKesson), operating two segments: (1) US Healthcare Solutions (distributes specialty pharmaceutical products, physician-administered drugs, and vaccines; operates the broadest specialty pharmacy network in the US); and (2) International Healthcare Solutions (distribution and manufacturer services in 14 countries including Europe, South America, and Canada). Cencora is particularly strong in HIV/AIDS therapeutics (serving ~50% of US patients), oncology, and orphan drugs. The company also provides third-party logistics (3PL) services for manufacturers and clinical trial support. Unlike McKesson, Cencora has minimal retail pharmacy exposure, making it a purer play on manufacturer and specialty services relationships.

financialSnapshot.text: Price $320.82 Market Cap $14.2bn P/E Ratio N/A EPS (TTM) N/A 52w High $377.54 15.0% from high 52w Low $271 18.4% above low Volume (avg) 1.0m

recentCatalysts.text: Specialty distribution market share gains in HIV/AIDS and oncology following Walgreens-SWS termination of the NowFern joint venture in 2024. European expansion through the /Cencora + Walgreens/ European procurement alliance driving supplier consolidation benefits. Manufacturer services revenue (clinical trial support, safety surveillance) growing ~22% annually as pharma R&D spend increases. New 3-year manufacturer contracts signed with six of the top 20 global pharma companies for US distribution exclusivity. Data analytics platform launch (Cencora Insights) monetising supply chain data for manufacturer clients.
items: Specialty distribution market share gains in HIV/AIDS and oncology following Walgreens-SWS termination of the NowFern joint venture in 2024.
European expansion through the /Cencora + Walgreens/ European procurement alliance driving supplier consolidation benefits.
Manufacturer services revenue (clinical trial support, safety surveillance) growing ~22% annually as pharma R&D spend increases.
New 3-year manufacturer contracts signed with six of the top 20 global pharma companies for US distribution exclusivity.
Data analytics platform launch (Cencora Insights) monetising supply chain data for manufacturer clients.

thesisEvaluation.text: Bull Case $395 Upside: +23% | Weight: 30% Base Case $345 Upside: +8% | Weight: 50% Bear Case $285 Downside: -11% | Weight: 20% Cencora is a high-quality, under-the-radar healthcare distribution business with a strong moat in specialty pharma and an underappreciated manufacturer services franchise. The lack of a reported P/E reflects the company's debt-financed Linde acquisition structure and significant intangible amortisation — underlying earnings power is stronger than GAAP suggests. The EV/EBITDA multiple is the more appropriate valuation metric. The business is structurally advantaged as pharmacy benefit managers and manufacturers consolidate around fewer, larger distribution partners.

keyRisks.text: 1 Customer concentration risk — top 10 manufacturer clients account for approximately 40% of revenues; losing a major client would be materially damaging. 2 Opioid litigation exposure (shared industry risk with MCK and CVS) with state attorneys general suits ongoing. 3 Integration risk following the 2023 Linde Healthcare acquisition — achieving targeted synergies is not guaranteed. 4 IRA impact on manufacturer pricing for Medicare Part D could reduce branded drug volumes through the distribution network. 5 Thin analyst coverage and limited retail investor awareness create valuation inefficiency, which can cut both ways.

whoShouldOwn.text: Own: Healthcare institutional investors seeking specialty pharma exposure without retail pharmacy risk; those comfortable with limited public disclosure. | Avoid: You require transparent, comparable P/E and EPS data; you want dividend income (Cencora's yield is modest); you require high-liquidity positions.

recommendation.text: HOLD HOLD. Fundamental quality is solid. Long-term investors can accumulate on dips below $290. The absence of P/E data is a yellow flag for due-diligence purposes — await FY2025 10-K filing for updated normalised earnings. Entry framework: scale in over 3 tranches on weakness.

entryExit.text: Long-term investors can accumulate on dips below $290.
